C1_LR_GOVERNANCE.note: Overcorrection gate (overcorrection exact mean 0) failed: P3 overcorrects more than mean P2.

C1_DESCRIPTIVE_SEMANTIC_SUBCLAIM.allowed_wording: MI removal improves leak-column recall and directional gap reduction over matched random removal, but these improvements do not satisfy the joint semantic-repair gate because overcorrection increases.
C1_DESCRIPTIVE_SEMANTIC_SUBCLAIM.forbidden_wording: MI removal is a pure unambiguously semantic repair operation.

C7_M09_SEMANTIC_GROUP.allowed_wording: At the 20% encoded-column budget, MI improves M09 semantic-group any-hit localization, but neither MI nor random deletion can remove the complete eight-column source group; full-group repair is not corroborated.
C7_M09_SEMANTIC_GROUP.forbidden_wording: M09 is repaired at the semantic-group level.

C2_LEGACY_SDR_LEARNER_INTERACTION.allowed_wording: No reliable learner interaction was detected for the legacy-SDR P3-minus-random contrast.
C2_LEGACY_SDR_LEARNER_INTERACTION.forbidden_wording: The governance effect is learner-invariant or equivalent across all learners for all metrics.
C2_LEGACY_SDR_LEARNER_INTERACTION.note: This claim is scoped to legacy SDR; directional_repair shows a small LR disadvantage.

global_limitations: C1 is SCORE_RECOVERY_ONLY: the overcorrection gate (exact mean 0) failed for all three learners.
Semantic evidence (leak recall, directional repair, M09 any-hit localization) is descriptively positive but is reported as independent subclaims, not as confirmatory repair corroboration.
FR4 conditional prevalence among keys with positive SDR: LR 47.6%, RF 48.3%, LightGBM 51.0%.
FR2 non-zero for RF/LightGBM: positive SDR can occur without removing any oracle-labeled leak column.
M09 full semantic-group repair is not corroborated at the 20% encoded-column budget.
